title: The Mangrove Guardians
text: Mangrove forests protect coastal communities from strong waves and storm surges. Their roots hold the soil together, reduce erosion, and create safe spaces for fish, crabs, and other marine life. When students learn how mangroves support both nature and people, they begin to understand why conservation projects are important in coastal barangays.
short_answer_prompt: Why are mangrove forests important to both people and marine animals?

title: A Morning at the Community Library
text: Lena arrived early at the community library with a notebook, a bottle of water, and a list of questions from her science class. She searched the shelves for books about volcanoes, copied useful facts, and asked the librarian for help when she found unfamiliar words. Before leaving, she organized her notes so she could explain her research clearly to her classmates the next day.
short_answer_prompt: How did Lena prepare herself to explain her research clearly?

title: Why Clean Water Matters
text: Every family depends on clean water for drinking, cooking, washing, and staying healthy. When rivers and canals are filled with trash, communities face more sickness, stronger foul odors, and fewer safe places for children to play. Protecting water sources through proper waste disposal and community clean-up drives is a practical responsibility that benefits everyone.
short_answer_prompt: What community actions can help protect clean water sources?
